title: Russia Launches 150 Drone Attack on Ukraine Overnight, Hitting Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia
summary: Russia attacked Ukraine with 150 drones overnight May 1-2, targeting Donetsk, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts. Ukrainian air defenses neutralized 126 of the incoming unmanned aerial vehicles.
contents:
## Massive Russian Drone Barrage Targets Eastern and Southern Ukraine

### Attack Details
Starting at 21:30 on May 1, Russian forces launched 150 Shahed UAVs and decoy drones from Bryansk, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Cape Chauda in occupied Crimea, according to Ukraine's Air Force. The attack targeted Donetsk, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts.

### Air Defense Response
Ukrainian air defense forces confirmed the downing of 64 Shahed attack drones and other types by 08:00 local time. An additional 62 Russian decoy drones disappeared from radar without causing adverse effects. Aircraft, anti-aircraft missile units, electronic warfare units, and mobile fire groups participated in repelling the assault.

### Casualties and Damage
In Zaporizhzhia, the number of people injured in drone strikes rose to 29, according to the Oblast Military Administration. Russian forces also struck a locomotive repair plant in the city of Zaporizhzhia, Ukrzaliznytsia reported.

### Broader Context
This attack followed a 210-drone barrage the previous night that struck Odesa, injuring 20 people and damaging residential buildings, a kindergarten, and a hotel. Ukraine continued its counter-offensive against Russian energy infrastructure, hitting the Tuapse port oil refinery for the fourth time in two weeks.

### Sources
Ukraine's Air Force, Ukrainska Pravda, and Ukrinform provided primary reporting.
